hard.next_step: 75% confidence 06 JUL - Completed POC planning meeting with Domino's Europe team. Next step is Domino's providing data for Spend POC. Some concerns about executing POC with disparate DPE resources, will work with Jai on this. 22 JUN - POC planning underway, we are waiting for Jai to respond / delegate data gathering for Spend. Should meet up this week in Brisbane. 6th Jun Clarification Q's from Jai (CPO) about the POC metrics and outcomes- what data do we require to run a successful POC etc 29 MAY - Post demo, prospect wants a POC to demonstrate capability and gain internal signoff. Plan for an in principal agreement to award post POC 26 MAY - Demo scheduled for 3rd June 11 <ay - we're in contact and working on a date late May 5th May Spoke to Jai who has asked for a demo to his team as next steps- he is aligning/coaching his team and will revert back to us early May 22nd Apr- Jai Ratongi (CPO) who is ex Thai Union (close to Carl) wants fast eval cycle for ANA- maybe, a mini POC to show stakeholders Wants to show quick value - and extend to CLM this year

forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is the honest category for a POC-gated Formal Evaluation, so the category is defensible; the 28 Aug 2026 close date is not. The POC has not started (spend data still owed as of the 6 Jul 2026 next step), and POC execution, CFO review, an in-principle award and contracting cannot fit in 50 days. This is a date problem on an otherwise healthy, engaged deal: move the close date to Q4 2026.
forecast_read.math: 50 days to close (2026-08-28) as of 2026-07-09. Opp created 2026-04-22, roughly 11 weeks in Formal Evaluation (no stage submission-date fields available for exact time-in-stage). The close date was pulled IN from 2026-10-30 to 2026-08-28 on 2026-05-29, and the amount rose 200,000 to 450,000 on 2026-06-06. Sequence still ahead: seven-market data extraction and normalization, POC build and playback, ANA scenario runs, internal signoff, in-principle award, then commercials and contracting. Forward pace required is roughly one milestone a week versus a current stall on buyer data delivery.

metrics.narrative: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) has clearly articulated the qualitative pain (procurement is 'bodies in spreadsheets'; a cheese spend roll-up takes two weeks to months; the CEO's technology-spend question has gone unanswered for 9 months, per the 3 Jun 2026 demo) and framed the business driver in hard terms on 3 Jun 2026: share price fell from $161 to $16.68 and the 'fit for growth' transformation is funded by taking cost out of procurement. Zycus surfaced illustrative quantified impact on the same demo (dairy consolidation savings of $126k-300k on dummy data, 80% paralegal efficiency in CLM, a 600% ROI expectation Jai himself repeated). None of these figures are yet Domino's-confirmed; the POC exists precisely to validate them on Domino's own data.
sources: Avoma call 29 May 2026 (Jai: cheese roll-up takes weeks)
Avoma demo 3 Jun 2026 (Jai: share price, cost-out mandate, CEO question; Zycus: savings illustrations)

economic_buyer.narrative: Lynn Carruthers (CFO) is named Executive Sponsor on the Salesforce contact roles, so the economic buyer is identified, but she has appeared on no call or email in the evidence read as of 9 Jul 2026. Funding logic is explicit but sits above the champion: Jai said on 3 Jun 2026 'ultimately we need to fund this, and the best way to fund it is by demonstrating that we can drive enhanced commercial outcomes from procurement,' and separately 'if the POC is very strong... George signs off' (a first name that matches no Salesforce contact, so the actual approval chain above Jai is unverified). Jai is building the ROI case on the economic buyer's behalf rather than the economic buyer engaging directly.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole (Lynn Carruthers, CFO, Executive Sponsor)
Avoma demo 3 Jun 2026 (funding and sign-off discussion)

decision_criteria.narrative: Jai laid out explicit priorities on 29 May 2026: first, spend visibility and analytics on Domino's own disparate data to uncover sourcing opportunities; second, a CLM to centralize contracts and cut leakage (today the repository sits in Jira with no workflow, per 3 Jun 2026); with an agentic AI overlay throughout. The 3 Jul 2026 Europe session hardened this into a concrete POC criterion set: iAnalyze proving value on 3 months of indirect spend data across seven markets (phase 1) plus ANA running about five defined tactical negotiation scenarios (phase 2). New evaluation criteria added on the calls: European hosting for group agreements (Fran, 3 Jun 2026) and supplier-language negotiation such as French (Antje, 3 Jul 2026).

decision_process.narrative: The buyer's stated process is demo, then a data-driven POC, then internal signoff, then 'an in principal agreement to award post POC' (next step entry, 29 May 2026). Jai personally sequences who joins when: finance and IT procurement at the 3 Jun 2026 demo, legal carved out for a separate CLM session with the chief legal officer (29 May and 3 Jun 2026), and Domino's Europe pulled in on 1-3 Jul 2026 with Michael Gengenbach named to lead the POC. The approval chain past Jai (CFO sign-off, IT alignment) has not been walked on any call, and no decision date is set for the POC readout.
sources: SF Next_Step__c (29 May and 06 Jul 2026 entries)

paper_process.narrative: First paper artifacts exist: DPE procurement (Kadie Skehan) issued an NDA on 26 May 2026 and returned it countersigned on 27 May 2026 (ITPP-9599), and Jai completed and returned the Source to Contract sizing and project estimate survey on 1 Jun 2026. A group-agreement contracting standard surfaced on 3 Jun 2026: Fran stated that for group agreements Domino's has historically required European hosting. MSA, Order Form, SOW and InfoSec mechanics have not been discussed on any read call, which is normal at Formal Evaluation ahead of a POC-gated award.
sources: Avoma demo 3 Jun 2026 (European hosting for group agreements)

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is extensively and specifically articulated by the pain owner, Jai: procurement maturity is 'exceptionally low... bodies in spreadsheets' with no common taxonomy across disparate ERPs (29 May 2026); indirect spend is largely unmanaged and CLM is absent with 'significant contract leakage' (29 May 2026); a zero-based budgeting exercise left indirects with 'very little understanding' and mis-mapped GL/cost centers (3 Jun 2026). The 3 Jul 2026 Europe call compounded it regionally: 12 markets on 12 different system setups, some markets with no ERP, and Germany still in D365 hypercare. The pain is tied to a board-level driver: cost-out to fund the transformation after the share-price fall (3 Jun 2026).

champion.narrative: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO, ex Thai Union, close to Carl Kimball per the 22 Apr 2026 next step) is a strong, active champion: he initiated the relationship at a Zycus event, requested the demo and 'coached' his team ahead of it (5 May 2026), sequenced the demo-POC-award plan himself (29 May 2026), told Zycus it has 'a front run... before we do anything formal' (29 May 2026), and kept the deal moving while traveling by delegating POC execution to his EU team (25 Jun 2026) and naming Michael to lead it (1 Jul 2026). What developed since the last read: the deal is no longer single-threaded on his calendar. What is missing: no second advocate at CFO/CIO level, so the commercial case still rides on him.
sources: Jai Rastogi emails 25 Jun and 1 Jul 2026 (SF-logged)

competition.narrative: This is a sole-source evaluation: Jai stated on 29 May 2026 that Zycus has 'a front run' before anything formal, and no rival has been invited in as of 9 Jul 2026. Coupa, SAP Ariba and Ivalua surface only as platforms Jai deployed in prior roles (3 Jun 2026), and Sirion and Icertis only as CLM tools he compared at Thai Union (29 May 2026): background credibility markers, not active bids. The real rival is the status quo: if the POC underwhelms, Domino's keeps running procurement on spreadsheets and a Jira contract repository and the cost-out program loses its identified fuel.
sources: Prior sweep record (29 May 2026 sole-source statement)

critical_signals.text: The proof of concept that gates Jai's in-principle award has not started: Domino's still owes the spend data (flagged again 6 Jul), and only 50 days remain to the 28 Aug close.
Domino's Europe team took its first Zycus session on 3 Jul and left positive, with Michael calling it 'a very interesting demo' and 'an exciting project'.
Jai widened the deal beyond himself on 1 Jul, naming Michael Gengenbach in Europe to lead the POC, though the new owners are openly worried about extracting data from 12 different market systems.
Still a sole-source evaluation: no rival has been invited in, and Jai has said Zycus has a front run before anything formal.
Two new proof points entered scope on 3 Jul: negotiating in the supplier's own language (French was asked) and bilateral re-negotiation with locked-in suppliers.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A genuinely live, sole-source, champion-led Formal Evaluation: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) is sequencing a POC-to-award path and has multi-threaded the work into Domino's Europe (session held 3 Jul 2026). Position is strong on access and preference, weak on proof and power: the POC that gates the award has not started because Domino's has not delivered spend data, the CFO has never appeared in the evidence read, and 50 days remain to a close date the remaining steps cannot fit. Trajectory steady versus the last sweep; the single likeliest blocker to the close is buyer data delivery.

win_position.text: Sole-source evaluation with a strong champion: Jai Rastogi (Group CPO) told Zycus on 29 May 2026 it has 'a front run' before anything formal, and no rival has been invited in since.

The buyer is spending real effort: a two-hour Brisbane demo with seven-plus attendees (3 Jun 2026), an in-person POC scoping with Jai (9 Jun 2026), and a Europe scoping session Jai arranged (3 Jul 2026).
Power is unproven: Lynn Carruthers (CFO, Executive Sponsor in the contact roles) has appeared on no call or email, and the sign-off chain above Jai referenced on 3 Jun 2026 is unmapped.
The award is conditional on a POC that has not started: Domino's had not delivered any spend data as of the 6 Jul 2026 next step, and its own team flagged extraction worries on 3 Jul 2026.

deal_momentum.text: Buyer responsiveness is high: Michael Gengenbach asked for and joined a session within 24 hours (2-3 Jul 2026) and requested ANA conversation trails to study afterward.
Jai kept the deal moving while traveling: he delegated POC data gathering to his EU team on 25 Jun 2026 and named Michael to lead the POC on 1 Jul 2026.
Data delivery is the bottleneck: the 6 Jul 2026 next step records the spend data still owed plus concerns about disparate DPE resources, and Luke's 8 Jul 2026 follow-up to Jai is awaiting reply.

customer_commitment.text: Jai committed a POC-to-award path on 29 May 2026: 'Plan for an in principal agreement to award post POC.'
DPE countersigned the NDA within a day (27 May 2026, ITPP-9599 via procurement's Kadie Skehan) and Jai returned the completed sizing survey on 1 Jun 2026.
Commitment is directional, not scheduled: as of 9 Jul 2026 no buyer-owned dates exist for data delivery, the POC readout, or a decision.

deal_risk.text: 50 days to the 28 Aug 2026 close cannot absorb seven-market data collection, POC execution, CFO review and contracting; the date, not the deal, is the problem.
POC execution depends on extracting data from 12 disparate market systems (some with no ERP, Germany still in D365 hypercare), a worry Antje and Dani voiced themselves on 3 Jul 2026.
The commercial case is single-threaded through Jai: if the POC underwhelms or he is pulled away, no CFO or CIO sponsor is built (both unengaged as of 9 Jul 2026).

customer_preference.evidence: Buyer-voiced: Jai gave Zycus a sole-source 'front run... before we do anything formal' (29 May 2026) and set a plan for 'an in principal agreement to award post POC' (next step, 29 May 2026); his EU team called the 3 Jul 2026 session 'a very interesting demo... an exciting project' (Michael Gengenbach).

business_case.evidence: The funding logic is explicit but unproven: Jai said on 3 Jun 2026 'we need to fund this, and the best way to fund it is by demonstrating that we can drive enhanced commercial outcomes from procurement,' with a 600% ROI expectation repeated by him on the same call. No Domino's-validated numbers exist yet; the POC on 3 months of seven-market indirect spend is the vehicle to produce them.

deal_movement.summary: Trajectory steady versus the last sweep: the evaluation stayed live through Jai's travel and multi-threaded into Europe, and the POC materials are now in the buyer's hands, but the POC itself has not started. The single likeliest blocker to the close is Domino's delivering the spend-data extracts, owed since the 9 Jun 2026 scoping and re-flagged on 6 Jul 2026.

items.change: Luke followed up with Jai for feedback on the Europe session and confirmed POC materials were provided; awaiting reply
Europe POC-scoping session held with Michael, Antje and Dani; POC templates, scope deck and ANA conversation trails shared the same day
Jai named Michael Gengenbach (DPE Europe) to lead the POC
Jai delegated POC data gathering to his EU team while traveling, with interim details promised from his AU team
In-person POC scoping with Jai; goal set to cover spend data across seven countries with iAnalyze plus ANA focus
Amount raised from 200,000 to 450,000 by Carl Kimball
Two-hour full-platform demo at Brisbane HQ to Jai and the indirect and IT procurement team; POC announced to the team
Close date pulled in from 2026-10-30 to 2026-08-28; NDA countersigned by DPE procurement two days earlier (ITPP-9599)

day_summary.overall: A follow-up day, not a meeting day. Luke Dougherty emailed Jai Rastogi recapping the previous Friday's session with the Domino's Europe team (Michael, Antje and Dani), noting it seemed to go well, confirming the POC materials had been provided, and asking what internal feedback Jai has heard. The ball now sits with Jai's team on feedback and the spend-data delivery.

items.name: Follow-up to Jai on the Europe POC session
items.summary: Recap of the 3 Jul session with Michael, Antje and Dani; POC materials confirmed provided; internal feedback requested from Jai.

competitive_position.summary: Sole-source evaluation: Jai gave Zycus 'a front run... before we do anything formal' (29 May 2026) and no formal competitive process is running as of 9 Jul 2026. Coupa, SAP Ariba and Ivalua are referenced only as platforms Jai personally deployed in prior roles (3 Jun 2026), and Sirion and Icertis only as CLM tools he assessed at Thai Union (29 May 2026): background credibility markers, not active bids. The strongest current threat is not a vendor but do-nothing: if the POC fails to produce a tangible proof point on Domino's own data, the company simply keeps running procurement on spreadsheets and a Jira contract repository, and the in-principle award never converts.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: Previous roles deployed Coupa and Ariba across organizations
competitors.how_we_win: No active evaluation exists; keep the conversation sole-source by protecting Jai's internal ROI narrative rather than inviting a competitive bake-off.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Previous roles deployed Coupa and Ariba across organizations
competitors.how_we_win: Same as Coupa: a background credibility marker for Jai, not an active bid.

competitors.name: Ivalua
competitors.quote: I've launched or deployed Ivalua, Coupa and Ariba throughout my life
competitors.source: Avoma call 3 Jun 2026 (Jai on prior-role deployments)
competitors.how_we_win: Same background context; no action needed beyond maintaining sole-source status.

competitors.name: Sirion
competitors.quote: when I was at Thai Union... we were looking at Sirion and Icertis and you
competitors.source: Avoma call 29 May 2026 (Jai on his prior CLM review)
competitors.how_we_win: Background CLM comparison only; win the dedicated CLM session with the chief legal officer on Word-native redlining and the single-platform story.

competitors.name: Icertis
competitors.quote: when I was at Thai Union... we were looking at Sirion and Icertis and you
competitors.source: Avoma call 29 May 2026 (Jai on his prior CLM review)
competitors.how_we_win: Same as Sirion: prior-role comparison, not an active bid at Domino's.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Expectations centre on proof against Domino's own messy multi-market data and on agentic AI doing real procurement work; Zycus is aligned on both, with two open proof points (European hosting detail and ANA language coverage) still to confirm.

best_practice_check.summary: Sole-source access and a strong champion are in hand; the win now depends on POC discipline and building sponsorship above Jai before the award moment.
flags: Convert the in-principle award into a mutual plan: agreed POC success criteria, buyer-owned data-delivery dates and a decision date with Jai (his 29 May 2026 intent still has no dates attached).
Multi-thread above the champion: use the POC readout to bring Lynn Carruthers (CFO) in; funding sign-off sits above Jai (3 Jun 2026) and no second executive sponsor is built.
De-risk data collection: start phase 1 with Australia plus one EU category, Antje's own suggestion on 3 Jul 2026, so first insight lands before seven-market extraction completes.

champion_strength.summary: Jai Rastogi is a strong, proactive champion: he initiated the relationship at a Zycus event, personally sequenced the demo-POC-award plan (29 May 2026), coached his team ahead of stakeholder reviews (5 May 2026), and kept the deal moving through six weeks of travel by delegating POC execution to his EU team (25 Jun 2026) and naming Michael Gengenbach to lead it (1 Jul 2026). Still missing is any advocate beyond Jai at CFO/CIO level, so his sponsorship alone carries the commercial case.
champion_strength.source: Avoma calls 29 May, 3 Jun and 3 Jul 2026; SF-logged emails 25 Jun-1 Jul 2026; SF Next_Step__c 22 Apr 2026

expansion_context.note: New-logo evaluation: no sibling Closed-Won evidence in the data read. The relationship originated at a Zycus event (Opportunity_Source__c set to Ground Events, 23 Apr 2026) via Jai's prior familiarity with Zycus from Thai Union.

scope_change.to: 450,000 group-wide scope with a POC spanning seven markets including Europe
scope_change.detail: Amount raised 200,000 to 450,000 by Carl Kimball on 6 Jun 2026 (field history). POC goal set on 9 Jun 2026 to cover spend data across seven countries (email recap 10 Jun 2026), and Jai pulled Domino's Europe into execution on 1-3 Jul 2026. Product scope on the record spans S2C modules plus ANA, Merlin Intake and iRisk; Jai's stated sequence remains spend analytics and ANA first, CLM next ('extend to CLM this year', 22 Apr 2026 next step).

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The deal is AI-led by the buyer's own design: Jai chose to engage after seeing agentic AI at a Zycus event, the POC's two proof points are iAnalyze spend intelligence and ANA autonomous negotiation, and the EU team asked to study ANA's historical conversation trails (3 Jul 2026). AI is the wedge, not an add-on.

ai_fit_signal.summary: Jai on 3 Jun 2026: 'I was excited when I saw your AI capabilities' as the way to 'leapfrog' Domino's procurement maturity; from first contact he wanted a fast evaluation cycle for ANA (22 Apr 2026 next step). The wider team is curious but pragmatic, probing agent language coverage, bilateral scenarios and supplier reactions to negotiating with an agent (3 Jul 2026).

items.detail: 28 Aug 2026 close is unreachable: the POC has not started (data owed as of 6 Jul 2026) and POC execution, CFO review, in-principle award and contracting all remain; close date was pulled in to 28 Aug on 29 May 2026
items.source: SF field history 2026-05-29; Next_Step__c 06 Jul 2026; Avoma call 3 Jul 2026

items.detail: POC data extraction spans 12 market system setups (some markets with no ERP, Germany in D365 hypercare, Netherlands on Navision); Antje voiced worry about the data ask and Dani must first check what Navision can extract
items.source: Avoma call 3 Jul 2026; Next_Step__c 06 Jul 2026 ('disparate DPE resources')

items.detail: No engagement above the champion: Lynn Carruthers (CFO) absent from all evidence, Wayne McMahon (CIO) unengaged, and the funding sign-off Jai referenced on 3 Jun 2026 is unmapped to a verified contact
items.source: SF contact roles; Avoma demo 3 Jun 2026

items.detail: No buyer-voiced budget exists for the 450,000 scope; the amount was set internally on 6 Jun 2026, and Domino's funding narrative depends on the POC proving cost-out value first
items.source: SF field history 2026-06-06; Avoma demo 3 Jun 2026 (funding via demonstrated outcomes)

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is warm and two-way: the buyer replies within days even while traveling, requested a session on 24 hours' notice, and volunteered candidate POC scenarios. The one-day-old silence on Luke's 8 Jul 2026 feedback request is normal cadence, not cooling.
items: Michael Gengenbach turned a cold introduction into a booked session within 24 hours (2-3 Jul 2026) and asked for follow-up materials to study
Jai replied from travel on 16 Jun and 25 Jun 2026 and named a POC lead on 1 Jul 2026 rather than letting the thread drop
DPE procurement countersigned the NDA in one day (26-27 May 2026) and Jai returned the sizing survey within days (1 Jun 2026)

items.action: Get Jai to name data owners and delivery dates, starting with Australia plus one EU category.

items.expected_effect: Starts the POC data pipeline and removes the single dependency blocking everything downstream; narrowing scope answers Antje's 3 Jul feasibility worry.

items.action: Agree POC success criteria and a decision date with Jai before data loading begins.
items.trigger: Jai's 29 May 2026 in-principle-award intent still has no success criteria or decision date attached
items.expected_effect: Converts an open-ended POC into a gated close plan and gives the 28 Aug close-date conversation an honest basis.

items.expected_effect: Locks POC phase 2 scope while phase 1 data is gathered, and answers Michael's bilateral-negotiation and Antje's language questions inside real scenarios.

items.action: Have Jai book a CFO readout of POC results with Lynn Carruthers before the award step.
items.trigger: Funding sign-off sits above Jai (3 Jun 2026) and the CFO has never engaged
